executive_summary.description: A high-level summary of the research findings and the final recommendations for designing a v1 Intermediate Representation (IR) for the Claude Code plugin. This section synthesizes the key takeaways from the analysis of all prior-art models.

v1_ir_borrowing_recommendations.description: A detailed answer to which prior-art IR schemas and concepts the v1 IR should borrow from directly. This includes a synthesis of the best features from technologies like SCIP, Kythe, and LSP.

obsolete_prior_art_evaluation.description: An evaluation of which prior-art technologies are considered obsolete or not worth studying in-depth for the purpose of this 2026 IR design, along with justifications for these conclusions (e.g., LSIF being superseded by SCIP).

source_technology.description: The prior-art technology from which a concept is being borrowed (e.g., SCIP, LSP, Kythe).

concept_to_import.description: The specific structure, field, or concept to be imported (e.g., Symbol.Descriptor, SymbolKind enum).

target_ir_application.description: How the imported concept will be used in the new v1 IR (e.g., for node IDs, for symbol type classification).

rationale.description: The justification for importing this specific concept.

technology_name.description: The name of the prior-art technology being analyzed.

data_model_shape.description: Description of the technology's fundamental data model (e.g., graph, stream, AST-annotated).

node_identity_strategy.description: The method used to uniquely and stably identify nodes or symbols (e.g., SCIP Symbol Descriptors, Kythe VNames).

edge_typing.description: How relationships between code entities (e.g., definitions, references) are typed and represented.

serialization_format.description: The data serialization format used (e.g., Protobuf, JSON Lines, XML).

versioning_strategy.description: The approach to schema versioning and ensuring compatibility.

maintenance_status_2026.description: The maintenance status and level of community or corporate support as of 2026.

llm_tool_calling_alignment.description: Analysis of the technology's suitability for use in LLM tool-calling contexts, considering factors like compactness and determinism.

prior_art_technologies_analysis.description: A detailed, in-depth analysis of each of the ten specified prior-art data models. Each item in the array will cover one technology, detailing its data model shape, node-identity strategy, edge typing, versioning, serialization format, maintenance status, and alignment with 2026-era LLM-tool-calling data shapes. The technologies covered are: SCIP, LSIF, LSP (CallHierarchyItem/DocumentSymbol), tree-sitter-stack-graphs, Kythe, GitHub Semantic, srcML, Graphviz DOT, Mermaid/D2, and the Nuanced post-mortem.

comparison_of_data_models.description: A comparative analysis of the different data model shapes (e.g., graph, stream, AST-annotated, XML-based) employed by the key technologies like SCIP, LSIF, Kythe, and srcML, highlighting their respective strengths and weaknesses.

comparison_of_node_identity_strategies.description: A comparative analysis of the various strategies for establishing stable, cross-language node identity. This focuses on the technical details of SCIP's Symbol Descriptors, Kythe's VNames, and the range-based identity in LSP.

comparison_of_edge_typing_and_relationsh.description: An analysis comparing how different models represent relationships between code entities. This covers the explicit edge types in SCIP, LSIF, and Kythe for concepts like 'defines', 'references', 'implements', and 'contains'.

comparison_of_serialization_and_versioni.description: A comparative overview of the serialization formats (e.g., Protobuf, JSON Lines, XML) and versioning disciplines used by the different protocols, discussing trade-offs in terms of size, performance, and schema evolution.

comparison_of_maintenance_and_ecosystem_.description: A summary of the current maintenance status, production usage, and tooling ecosystem maturity for each technology as of 2026. This includes information on which projects are actively maintained, archived, or have niche adoption.

analysis_of_llm_tool_calling_alignment.description: An evaluation of how well each data model aligns with the requirements of 2026-era LLM tool-calling. This considers factors such as compactness of the data shape, determinism, streamability, and ease of schema evolution.

data_model_shape.description: Overview of SCIP's data model, including Document, Occurrence, and Symbol messages.

node_identity_strategy.description: Detailed explanation of the SCIP Symbol.Descriptor grammar (scheme.manager.package.descriptor) and its stability.

edge_typing_and_relationships.description: Description of how SCIP's Relationship messages capture definitions, references, and implementations.

serialization_and_versioning.description: Information on the protobuf serialization, streaming model, and schema evolution practices.

tooling_ecosystem_maturity.description: Evaluation of the official language indexers (scip-typescript, scip-python, etc.) and their maintenance status.

production_usage_and_performance.description: Details on SCIP's usage at Sourcegraph and its performance benefits over LSIF.

lsif_data_model.description: Description of LSIF's JSONL graph model with vertex and edge types.

key_pain_points.description: Summary of LSIF's limitations, such as large file sizes, high memory usage, and schema rigidity.

concepts_retained_in_scip.description: Valuable concepts from LSIF that were carried forward into the design of SCIP.

lessons_for_new_ir_design.description: Actionable lessons for designing a new IR, based on the LSIF-to-SCIP transition (e.g., prefer typed schemas, binary serialization).

typescript_schema_summary.description: A summary of the precise TypeScript-friendly schemas for DocumentSymbol, CallHierarchyItem, and SymbolKind.

editor_rendering_behavior.description: Explanation of how editors like VS Code interpret and render these LSP data structures into UI elements like outlines and call trees.

ir_alignment_recommendations.description: Specific recommendations for how the v1 IR should align its field names and enums with LSP to maximize compatibility.

naming_and_extension_tradeoffs.description: Analysis of the pros and cons of reusing LSP names versus extending the IR with custom fields like a stable UID.

recommended_dot_attribute_subset.description: A list of the canonical Graphviz DOT attributes (label, shape, style, color, etc.) that should be adopted verbatim.

mermaid_mapping_guidelines.description: Guidelines on how to map the IR's node and edge properties to Mermaid's internal AST for rendering.

d2_model_mapping_guidelines.description: Guidelines on how to map the IR's properties to the documented model of the D2 diagramming language.

shared_visualization_schema_proposal.description: A proposal for a shared, normalized visualization schema layer within the IR to ensure consistent rendering across different tools.

evaluation_summary.description: A summary of Nuanced's evaluation of tools like pycg, tree-sitter-stack-graphs, Glean, and scip-python.

jarvis_selection_rationale.description: The key reasons why Nuanced chose the JARVIS IR, focusing on stability, practicality, and performance.

lessons_for_ir_design.description: Cross-cutting, actionable lessons for the v1 IR design derived from the post-mortem's findings.

identified_risks_and_open_questions.description: Risks and open questions highlighted by the post-mortem, relevant to the design of a new IR.
